pattern: A shortest-path search over (city, time-elapsed) states minimizes total fees subject to the time budget.
hints: The state needs both where you are and how much time you have spent so far.
Track the cheapest fee to reach each city at each total time, and only expand states within the time budget.
Run a Dijkstra-like search keyed on accumulated cost, relaxing neighbors when the new time stays within maxTime, and answer the cheapest arrival at the last city.

edge_inputs.description: anchor example cheap path fits in time
anchor example tighter time forces costlier path
direct edge too slow but no alternative
multiple parallel edges pick the fast one
longer hop count but lower fees within time

stress.description: random connected-ish graph with small node count and bounded edges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, size))
    fees = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        j = rng.randrange(i)
        edges.append([i, j, rng.randint(1, 20)])
    extra = rng.randint(0, n)
    for _ in range(extra):
        a = rng.randrange(n)
        b = rng.randrange(n)
        if a != b:
            edges.append([a, b, rng.randint(1, 20)])
    maxTime = rng.randint(1, 200)
    return [maxTime, edges, fees]
